result.root_cause: Batch size configuration change on Jan 10 from 50 to 100 is causing memory pressure and performance degradation. The doubled batch size exceeds available memory capacity, causing: (1) memory usage to spike to 92% (vs 65% baseline), (2) GC time to increase 5.6x (450ms vs 80ms), (3) message queue backlog to grow 5x (5000 vs 1000), and (4) worker throughput to drop by 44% (45 vs 80). This creates a cascading effect where workers cannot process messages fast enough, leading to queue buildup and gradual P99 latency degradation (15.5% drift over 20 days). This is NOT a memory leak but a resource configuration mismatch.

fix_options.title: Revert Batch Size to Previous Stable Value
commands: kubectl set env deployment/worker-service BATCH_SIZE=50 -n production
fix_options.rollback: kubectl set env deployment/worker-service BATCH_SIZE=100 -n production && kubectl rollout status deployment/worker-service -n production

fix_options.title: Scale Worker Instances Horizontally
commands: kubectl get pods -l app=worker-service -n production -o wide
kubectl set env deployment/worker-service BATCH_SIZE=75 -n production
sleep 120 && kubectl logs -l app=worker-service -n production --tail=100 | grep -E 'memory|throughput|queue'
fix_options.rollback: kubectl scale deployment/worker-service --replicas=3 -n production && kubectl set env deployment/worker-service BATCH_SIZE=100 -n production

fix_options.title: Tune Batch Size with JVM Heap Adjustment
commands: kubectl get configmap worker-service-config -n production -o yaml > /tmp/worker-config-backup.yaml
kubectl patch configmap worker-service-config -n production --type merge -p '{"data":{"BATCH_SIZE":"60","JAVA_OPTS": "-Xmx4g -Xms4g -XX:+UseG1GC -XX:MaxGCPauseMillis=200"}}'
for i in {1..5}; do sleep 60; kubectl top pods -l app=worker-service -n production; done
fix_options.rollback: kubectl apply -f /tmp/worker-config-backup.yaml && kubectl rollout restart deployment/worker-service -n production && kubectl rollout status deployment/worker-service -n production
